possibly cargo-deny for license/dependency checks","error_handling":"thiserror-based custom error enums per module (e.g. ForgeError, ScannerError), anyhow used at binary boundary for error context propagation"},"patterns":["Trait-based abstraction (Forge trait) with per-provider struct implementations, pattern already partially present for GitHub","Enum-based Provider dispatch to select forge implementation at runtime based on config or detected remote URL","Error enums with thiserror wrapping underlying HTTP/client errors, converted to anyhow at CLI boundary","Async trait methods via async-trait crate for forge API calls given tokio runtime usage","Config-driven base URL and auth token resolution per forge, currently hardcoded for GitHub only"],"related_issues":[{"number":735,"title":"feat: initial GitHub forge abstraction layer","relevance":"Established the initial Forge trait and GitHub-only implementation that #1205 must extend to other forges"},{"number":736,"title":"PR implementing Forge trait and octocrab-backed GitHub client","relevance":"Reference implementation pattern to replicate for GitLab, Gitea/Forgejo, and Azure DevOps clients; maintainer likely expects consistent trait signatures"},{"number":1150,"title":"chore: add wiremock-based integration test harness for forge clients","relevance":"Test scaffolding that new forge implementations should reuse for consistent coverage"}],"constraints":["Must preserve existing Forge trait signature or version it carefully to avoid breaking GitHub implementation and its tests","Async runtime is tokio-based; new forge clients must use async-trait or native async fn in traits consistent with MSRV","Config schema changes must remain backward compatible for existing GitHub-only users (default provider = github)","Each new forge client should have equivalent mocked integration test coverage before merge, per project testing convention","Binary size / compile time growth should be considered if feature-gating per forge is desired"],"test_coverage":"GitHub forge has integration tests using mocked HTTP responses (wiremock) covering PR fetch and comment posting; SecurityScanner has unit tests for pattern matching logic independent of forge, but no existing tests exist for GitLab, Gitea/Forgejo, or Azure DevOps since those clients do not yet exist","library_findings":[{"library":"octocrab","version":"0.3x (per Cargo.toml pin)","relevant_api":"Octocrab client builder, pulls/comments endpoints","notes":"GitHub-specific; not reusable for other forges, confirms need for separate client crates or hand-rolled reqwest clients per forge"},{"library":"gitlab (crate) / reqwest","version":"gitlab crate ~0.16x or hand-rolled reqwest for GitLab REST v4 API","notes":"No official async-first maintained gitlab crate as mature as octocrab; likely better to implement thin reqwest-based client for GitLab, Gitea/Forgejo (which shares GitHub-like REST API), and Azure DevOps REST API rather than pull in heavyweight per-forge SDKs"},{"library":"async-trait","version":"0.1.x","notes":"Needed if Forge trait methods must remain object-safe with async fns for dyn dispatch across forge implementations; alternative is enum_dispatch to avoid async-trait boxing overhead"}],"approaches":[{"name":"Trait-based Forge abstraction with per-provider modules (extend existing pattern from #736)","description":"Extend the existing Forge trait (from GitHub implementation) with concrete GitLabForge, GiteaForge, and AzureDevOpsForge structs implementing the same trait via async-trait. Use reqwest directly for GitLab/Gitea/Azure since Gitea/Forgejo largely mirrors GitHub's REST shape and GitLab/Azure have well documented REST v4 APIs. Provider selection driven by config field or remote URL detection.","pros":["Follows established project convention from #735/#736, minimal architectural surprise","Incremental: one forge client can be added and tested at a time","Keeps SecurityScanner core untouched since it already depends on the Forge trait abstraction, not concrete GitHub types"],"cons":["async-trait adds small runtime boxing overhead per call","Some duplication of HTTP boilerplate across three new client modules unless shared helper is factored out","Requires careful handling of differing pagination/rate-limit semantics across forges"],"complexity":"medium","files_touched":9},{"name":"Unified generic REST adapter with forge-specific config maps","description":"Instead of separate structs per forge, build a single GenericForgeClient parameterized by a ForgeSpec (base URL templates, auth header format, JSON field mappings) loaded from static config per provider. Reduces code duplication by centralizing HTTP logic and only varying endpoint/auth specifics via data rather than trait implementations.","pros":["Less code duplication, single code path to test and maintain","Easier to add future forges (e.g. Bitbucket) by adding config data rather than new Rust modules","Smaller binary and simpler dependency graph (no need for multiple SDK crates)"],"cons":["Field mapping abstraction can become a leaky, hard-to-debug indirection layer when forge APIs diverge structurally (e.g. Azure DevOps uses very different resource model than GitHub-style REST)","Departs from the trait-per-forge pattern established in #736, may not match maintainer expectations","Higher upfront design complexity to get the abstraction right for divergent APIs like Azure DevOps"],"complexity":"complex","files_touched":6},{"name":"Feature-flagged forge crates behind Cargo features","description":"Split each forge implementation into its own module gated by a Cargo feature flag (github, gitlab, gitea, azure-devops), all implementing the shared Forge trait, with default features enabling only github + gitlab for smaller default builds. CI matrix builds with --all-features for full coverage.","pros":["Keeps compile times and binary size manageable for users who only need a subset of forges","Clear separation of concerns per forge, easy to test in isolation via feature-scoped test targets","Aligns with common Rust ecosystem convention for optional integrations"],"cons":["Adds CI matrix complexity (must test multiple feature combinations)","Slightly more boilerplate in Cargo.toml and conditional compilation attributes throughout forge module","Could complicate the Provider enum dispatch if a feature is disabled but referenced in config"],"complexity":"medium","files_touched":11}],"recommendation":"Approach 1 (trait-based Forge abstraction with per-provider modules) is recommended: it directly extends the pattern already established by #735/#736, minimizes risk to existing GitHub functionality and its test suite, and allows incremental delivery (e.g. ship GitLab first, then Gitea/Forgejo, then Azure DevOps) each with its own mocked integration tests following the existing wiremock convention. The feature-flag approach (3) is a reasonable follow-up refinement once all three clients exist, to address binary size concerns, but should not block the initial multi-forge implementation."}
